data.referralSource: Community Relations Bureau of the Commission
Community Relations Bureau of the Commission

catalog.name: NYC — Programmable City (reference API)
catalog.note: Static reference API — every endpoint is example data at a stable URL, served off GitHub Pages. Reads only; write operations are documented in the OpenAPI but not executed here. Data is synthesized for demonstration.

skills.name: Apply for a permit, license, or benefit
skills.description: Start and track an application to the city — a building permit, business or professional license, benefit (SNAP/cash/Medicaid), grant, school or college admission, or lottery.

skills.name: Report a problem or file a complaint
skills.description: File a 311-style service request or a complaint/tip with the agency that owns it — noise, a pothole, a building condition, a consumer or rights complaint, a tip to an investigator.

skills.name: Request public records
skills.description: Make a records request — a FOIL request, a vital record (birth/death/marriage), a certified document, or a transcript.

skills.name: Schedule or reserve
skills.description: Book an appointment, inspection, hearing slot, facility, or hold — an OATH hearing, a DOB inspection, a park/venue reservation, a library hold.

skills.name: Register or enroll
skills.description: Register or enroll with the city — register to vote or request a ballot, register a business or vendor, enroll in a program.

skills.name: Dispute or appeal
skills.description: Challenge a city determination — appeal a property-tax assessment, dispute a violation or ticket, respond to a summons, file a grievance.

skills.name: Pay a city charge
skills.description: Pay a fine, fee, tax, or bill owed to the city and get a receipt.

skills.name: Look up a property or place
skills.description: Resolve a NYC address, BBL, or BIN and return everything the city knows about that place across agencies — its districts, who represents it, and its records (buildings, housing, environment, complaints).

skills.name: Check application or case status
skills.description: Look up the status of an application, permit, complaint, case, or request across agencies.

skills.name: Search city data and records
skills.description: Search and read the city's open records and datasets — filings, permits, violations, licenses, budgets, contracts, results — the read surface across all 67 agencies.

Borough.description: One of the five NYC boroughs. Open Data extracts spell this many ways ('Borough', 'BOROUGH', 'BORO', numeric 1-5); the canonical value is the full name.

BoroCode.description: DCP borough code: 1=Manhattan, 2=Bronx, 3=Brooklyn, 4=Queens, 5=Staten Island. The leading digit of every BBL and BIN.

Coordinates.description: A WGS84 point. Open Data delivers these as text (gis_latitude/longitude) or number (latitude/longitude) depending on the extract. Some agencies (e.g. NYPD) also carry the NY State Plane projection (xCoordinate/yCoordinate).

xCoordinate.description: NY State Plane (Long Island zone) X, where an agency carries it instead of / alongside WGS84.

yCoordinate.description: NY State Plane (Long Island zone) Y.

CommunityDistrict.description: A NYC Community District (Community Board area). DCP-defined (xi7c-iiu2). Referenced by nearly every land-use, service, and complaint record.

borocd.description: Borough + district as a single code, e.g. 101 = Manhattan CD 1.

CouncilDistrict.description: City Council district (1-51). Boundaries published by DCP; the geography every Council dataset joins on.

CensusTract.description: 2020 US Census tract as maintained by DCP (63ge-mke6).

NTA.description: Neighborhood Tabulation Area (2020 vintage) — DCP-defined aggregation of census tracts used for neighborhood-level demographics.

AdminBoundaries.description: The administrative-district overlays that a NYC location falls within, beyond the core land-use geography — the service districts agencies use to route work. Any subset may be present; open (agencies carry additional overlays such as taxi/sanitation micro-districts).

GeographySpine.description: THE shared location block of NYC government — the recurring key set that stitches records across agencies. DCP attaches it to a place; Buildings, Finance, Housing, Planning, Parks, DOE, and 311 all implicitly carry it. Make this the base every domain `$ref`s. All fields optional (a record carries whatever geography it knows); open (`additionalProperties`) so an agency's extra overlays — taxiZone, evacuationZone, sanitation micro-district — are never rejected.

communityBoard.description: Community district / community board number (PLUTO `community board`).

communityDistrict.description: Alias some agencies use for the community district / board.

nta.description: Neighborhood Tabulation Area code (2020 vintage), e.g. 'MN0101'.

neighborhood.description: Neighborhood name where an agency carries a text label rather than an NTA code.

zip.description: ZIP / postal code of the location.

prompts.name: who_represents_this_address
prompts.description: Given a NYC address, return the council member, community board, borough, and every district that represents it.

prompts.name: everything_about_this_place
prompts.description: Everything the city knows about a BBL/BIN/address across agencies — buildings, housing, environment, complaints, landmarks.

prompts.name: how_do_i_apply_for
prompts.description: Explain how to apply for a given permit, license, or benefit and start the application with the owning agency.

prompts.name: report_a_problem_at
prompts.description: File a 311-style report or complaint for a location and route it to the owning agency.

prompts.name: whats_the_status_of_my
prompts.description: Look up the status of an application, permit, complaint, or case across agencies by its id.

prompts.name: what_agency_handles
prompts.description: Identify which NYC agency owns a given task or record and hand off to its tools.

prompts.name: find_a_service_near_me
prompts.description: Find city services or facilities near a location (parks, libraries, clinics, offices).
